title: rubus hirtus var. horridifactus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. horridus, also known as bramble or thistle, is a type of wild rose with thorny stems and clusters of small, white flowers. The plant is rich in flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids, which are known for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. horridus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of Rubus hirtus var. horridus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
